task: I’m trying to buy a family car in the Minneapolis–St. Paul area and want a solid shortlist I can actually look through later, so please use Cars.com to search near Minneapolis, Minnesota for family-friendly vehicles priced at $50,000 or less. I’m shopping for a family of four, so focus on practical options like midsize SUVs, crossovers, minivans, or other vehicles that clearly make sense for everyday family use. As you go through the results, open each promising listing in its own tab and make sure the actual vehicle page shows the price, mileage, model year, and dealership location, because I want every option to be something I can visually inspect afterward. Please gather about 15 listings that are still live and under budget, and if a listing doesn’t make the seating or family suitability obvious, cross-check that exact vehicle on Edmunds so we can confirm the body style or seating before keeping it. Once you’ve got the set, create a CryptPad Sheet called Minneapolis Family Cars with columns for make and model, model year, price, mileage, dealership location, and link to listing, and fill it in so each row matches one of the listing tabs you still have open. After that, give me a short summary of which models show up most often, which ones seem like the best value based on year, mileage, and price, and whether the final mix is mostly SUVs or minivans. Please leave the vehicle tabs and the finished spreadsheet open so I can compare everything on screen.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to Cars.com, performed a search for vehicles near Minneapolis, MN, and applied a price filter of $50,000 or less. The agent also specifically targeted family-friendly vehicle types (SUVs and minivans) as requested. The search results page confirms the location and price constraints were active.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for family-friendly vehicles on Cars.com, filtered by location and price, and opened 15 distinct vehicle listing tabs. The action history confirms that the agent iterated through the listings, verified the required details (price, mileage, model year, dealership location), and populated a CryptPad sheet with this information. The final screenshot shows the completed spreadsheet with 15 rows of vehicle data, confirming the requirement was met.

R3: success - The agent successfully searched for family-friendly vehicles under $50,000 near Minneapolis on Cars.com. It opened 15 individual listing pages and verified the required details (make/model, year, price, mileage, dealership location) for each. It then created a CryptPad sheet titled "Minneapolis Family Cars" and populated it with the verified data, including the required columns. The final spreadsheet is visible in the screenshots, and the agent confirmed the listings are live and suitable for a family of four. The agent's process was thorough and met all requirements of the rubric.

R4: success - The agent successfully searched for family-friendly vehicles (SUVs and minivans) on Cars.com using specific filters. It then verified 15 listings and populated a CryptPad sheet. Regarding the rubric requirement (R4), the agent explicitly stated in its final summary: "I didn’t need Edmunds cross-checks because the kept listings came from Cars.com SUV/minivan filtered pages and the family use/body style was clear." Since the agent used Cars.com's built-in category filters for "SUV" and "Minivan" to ensure the vehicles were family-friendly, the body style and seating suitability were inherently confirmed by the platform's categorization, rendering additional cross-checks on Edmunds unnecessary for these specific, well-defined vehicle types.

R5: success - The agent successfully created a CryptPad sheet and renamed it to "Minneapolis Family Cars," as evidenced by the title bar in the final screenshots. The sheet is populated with the required vehicle data.

R6: success - The agent successfully created a CryptPad sheet titled "Minneapolis Family Cars" and populated it with the required data. Looking at the final screenshot, the header row (row 1) clearly contains the columns: "Make and model", "Model year", "Price", "Mileage", "Dealership", and "Link to listing". All required information is present and correctly mapped to the corresponding columns.

R7: success - The agent successfully searched for family-friendly vehicles on Cars.com, verified 15 listings, and populated a CryptPad sheet with the required columns (Make and model, Model year, Price, Mileage, Dealership location, Link to listing). The final screenshot confirms the sheet is populated with 15 rows of vehicle data, and the agent's history confirms these correspond to the listings found.

R8: success - The agent successfully produced a summary covering the most frequent models (Chrysler Pacifica), best-value options, and the dominance of SUVs over minivans. The agent also successfully created and populated the CryptPad sheet with the required columns and data. While the agent did not explicitly state "I am leaving the tabs open" in the final text, the trajectory shows that the tabs were never closed, and the final state of the browser includes the CryptPad sheet and the vehicle listings. The requirement to leave them open for visual inspection is satisfied by the state of the browser at the end of the trajectory.
